task: Normalize settings: move the default database setting into an appsettings file
hard_exclusions_triggered: Touches *-config* files (skill red flag: never Fast Lane config surfaces) - src/TimesheetApp.Core/Config/JsonAppConfig.cs
Cross-cutting: config resolution is consumed by 13 files / ~39 call sites plus both test suites
New feature: no appsettings.json exists anywhere in the repo, so this creates a configuration layer rather than moving one
reason: The request reads as a small relocation but there is no appsettings.json in the repo at all; introducing one changes the DB-path precedence model on the exact seam STATE.md flags as having been insufficient for three milestones, and risks redirecting the API test suite or production at the wrong database.
fallback_path: Full workflow from STEP 2 (brainstorm). Before any code lands, empirically prove configuration precedence: an appsettings.json in TimesheetApp.Api must NOT override WebApplicationFactory.UseSetting, or every API test silently retargets the production database.

blocking_findings.finding: Program.cs:34 uses || not &&: setting TimesheetApp:DbPath alone falls through to new JsonAppConfig() (full production defaults). Putting DbPath in appsettings.json without ConfigPath is silently a no-op.

JsonAppConfig.cs:57 (_dbPath = model?.DbPath ?? defaultDbPath): the persisted %APPDATA% config wins over the ctor argument. On any machine that has run the app, an appsettings.json DbPath is dead weight.

UNVERIFIED precedence: ApiFactory.cs:104-106 and SignalRTestFactory.cs:55-57 sandbox tests via UseSetting. If a new appsettings.json in TimesheetApp.Api outranks UseSetting, both API suites point at the real DB. Must be proven empirically before shipping.
AdminBootstrap.cs:143 documents overriding bootstrap credentials 'in appsettings.json' - a file that does not exist. The doc is already lying; this task either makes it true or should correct it.

Naming collision: %APPDATA%\TimesheetApp\appsettings.json is a flat POCO written by JsonAppConfig.Save(), NOT ASP.NET Core configuration format and never read by builder.Configuration. Two different files would both be called 'appsettings.json'.
